C-VV-HANDOFF-SKILL.Summary: Add a packaged vv-handoff managed skill that writes session handoff XML notes under project-local .vvoc/handoff date-slug directories.
C-VV-HANDOFF-SKILL.Problem: Users need a lightweight end-of-session way to preserve the visible session context for a future session without running extra commands, creating a CLI command, or validating a formal schema.

Goals.Goal: Provide a bundled managed OpenCode skill named vv-handoff delivered by existing vvoc install and sync managed-skill mechanisms.
Define skill behavior that writes .vvoc/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD-<session-slug>/handoff.xml using an agent-derived slug and suffixes -2, -3, and so on on path collision.

Capture the current visible session context into required XML sections: original goal, completed work, current state and decisions, important or changed files, known commands/checks and results, blockers/risks/unknowns, and next safe step.
Require the skill to redact secrets before writing handoff.xml.

NonGoals.NonGoal: Do not add a separate vvoc CLI command, OpenCode plugin, runtime hook, or automatic end-of-session writer.
Do not create handoff.md or any non-XML canonical artifact.
Do not validate handoff.xml or introduce a formal schema for it.
Do not make vv-handoff run tests, lint, build, git status, git diff, or any other command for additional context collection.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: vv-handoff appears in the managed skill set and is installed/synced through existing managed skill delivery alongside vv-spec, vv-plan, vv-review, vv-execute, and vv-reflect.

The packaged skill instructions require writing only .vvoc/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD-<session-slug>/handoff.xml in the current project, with session-slug generated from the main session goal and collision handling by suffixing -2, -3, and later integers.
The skill instructions require using only current visible/known session context and explicitly forbid running commands or checks to collect fresh context.
If git status, git diff, or verification evidence was not already collected in the current session, the handoff records it as not collected in current session rather than collecting it.

The handoff XML contains required sections for original request, completed work, current state and decisions, important or changed files, known checks/commands and results, blockers/risks/unknowns, and next safe step.
The skill instructions require secret redaction before writing, replacing tokens, keys, passwords, cookies, private URLs, and similar sensitive values with [REDACTED].
README managed-skill documentation and GRACE graph metadata reflect vv-handoff as a bundled managed skill.
Managed-skill install/sync tests or assertions are updated so vv-handoff is covered by existing managed-skill delivery verification.

VerificationIntent.ExpectedEvidence: Managed-skill tests show vv-handoff is installed and synced through existing delivery paths; typecheck/lint/format pass; GRACE lint accepts the updated change, graph, and verification artifacts.

Assumptions.Assumption: handoff.xml is a free-form XML note for agents and humans, not a schema-validated artifact.
The skill writes to project-local .vvoc/handoff/ only; global handoff storage is out of scope.

Implementation can be mostly prompt-template based; TypeScript changes are limited to managed-skill registration, packaging delivery tests, and docs/metadata updates unless implementation evidence shows more is needed.
Existing vvoc managed skill reference-copy support may be used only if implementation chooses to include optional reference templates; it is not required by this spec.
Conversation history hidden by compaction or unavailable to the active agent is not reconstructed; the skill uses only visible/known context at invocation time.
